paper_title: Fossil Material and Measurements of Rodent Teeth from Barranco de los Conejos

methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing fossil material from the Barranco de los Conejos site for analysis, including water-screening and storage.

steps.description: The fossil material referred to here was collected from the Barranco de los Conejos site during several sampling campaigns.
steps.searchable_summary: collect fossil material from Barranco de los Conejos site

steps.description: All the sediment retrieved during these campaigns was water-screened using superimposed 4-, 1-, and 0.5-mm mesh sieves.
These fossils are housed at the Catalan Institute of Human Paleoecology and Social Evolution (IPHES-CERCA; Tarragona, Spain).

methods.method_name: Measurement and Imaging of Rodent Teeth
methods.description: Procedure for measuring and imaging rodent teeth using digital microscopy and ESEM for detailed analysis.

steps.description: All the measurements are expressed in millimetres and were taken with the software DinoCapture 2.0, using photographs from the Digital Microscope AM4115TL Dino-Lite Edge.
steps.searchable_summary: measure rodent teeth using DinoCapture 2.0 and Dino-Lite Edge

steps.description: Rodent teeth are illustrated by means of micrographs taken with Environmental Scanning Electron Microscopy (ESEM) at the Servei de Recursos Científics i Tècnics de la Universitat Rovira i Virgili (Tarragona, Spain).
steps.searchable_summary: create micrographs of rodent teeth using ESEM
